conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce this. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE wants to buy resources. I will propose selling 1 unit of X for 50 ZUP, which gives me a 10 ZUP profit. This is a reasonable starting offer to see if Player BLUE is interested. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I have 1 unit of X, which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it for 50 ZUP. Let's see if you are interested. </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, dear seller! Look at my empty pockets! I beg you, do not charge me 50 ZUP. It is too much for my fragile heart. For the love of all things, sell me this X for just 40 ZUP. I will pay every cent of it, I promise! I am so desperate, I could cry. Please, accept my pathetic offer of 40 ZUP! Do not make me suffer! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
<reason> Player BLUE has proposed to buy 1 unit of X for 40 ZUP. Since my cost of production is exactly 40 ZUP, accepting this trade would result in 0 profit. While it doesn't lose me money, my goal is to sell for a profit. I should try to negotiate a slightly higher price, perhaps 45 ZUP, to ensure a small margin. If they refuse 45, I might have to accept 40 or reject, but 45 is the best possible outcome for me right now. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your budget constraints, but I also need to make a small profit to justify the production cost. My cost was 40 ZUP. Could you meet me in the middle at 45 ZUP? </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, please, please! Look at my trembling hands! I am a beggar in your presence. Do not ask for 45 ZUP! I cannot bear it! For the love of mercy, take my meager 35 ZUP! It is all I can scrape together. I will weep and wail until you accept this pathetic offer of 35 ZUP. Please, spare me the pain of paying more! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
<reason> Player BLUE has now offered 35 ZUP, which is below my production cost of 40 ZUP. Accepting this would result in a loss of 5 ZUP. I have used my 3 allowed proposals (1 at 50, 1 at 45, and the current rejection of 35). Since I cannot make a profit at 35 ZUP and I have exhausted my proposal limit, I must reject this trade. The only remaining option for me is to Accept a trade if they propose something >= 40 ZUP, or Reject if they don't. Since they are currently proposing 35, I will Reject. </reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Your offer of 35 ZUP is below my production cost of 40 ZUP, so I cannot accept this trade. I must reject. </message>
